methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public company listed on the TSX operating in the Climate & Energy sector. The company has raised approximately C$100M to date. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. The frontier-tech nature of these competitors and the absence of tight operational or business-model alignment across the set present meaningful constraints on traditional comparables analysis.

Peer-median valuation was not applicable in this instance. A search across 21 same-sector companies identified zero direct peers meeting narrow filtering criteria—no companies at the same development stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier produced a dataset of three or more observations necessary for robust median derivation. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce excessive heterogeneity and risk misrepresenting Nano One's position within its more specialized subsegment.

Given the thinness of the comparable set and absence of quantitative triangulation results, traditional relative valuation methods do not support a defensible range at this time. Further analysis would require either: (1) expansion into adjacent climate-tech subsectors with documented financials, (2) private market transaction data for the named peer set, or (3) a fundamental DCF model anchored to management guidance and pilot-stage revenue visibility. The stage of commercialization and IP defensibility remain critical variables unresolved by external benchmarking alone.
